validation.url: Must be a valid http(s):// URL
validation.host: Must be a valid hostname or IP
validation.password_strength: At least 8 characters, with letters and digits
validation.form_has_errors: Please fix the highlighted fields first

metric.emergency_active_hint: Users inside an active emergency window

alerts.expiring_empty: No accounts expire in the next 7 days

traffic.title: Traffic leaderboard (current period)
traffic.empty: No traffic data yet (the first 5-minute collection window hasn't completed).

settings.title: System settings

scope.override_hint: Override the settings below for this group; un-overridden items inherit the global default.

settings.feature_pending: This tab is being migrated to React + MUI (next iteration). Use the Vue version meanwhile.

general.captcha_provider_image: Built-in image captcha (recommended, no external calls)
general.password_recovery_hint: Requires SMTP email to be configured. Only applies to accounts that have an email and a local password; SSO accounts are unaffected.
general.registration_hint: Registrants sign in with their email as the username, get the user role, join the default group, and inherit the quota/expiry above. Email verification needs SMTP configured; link delivery needs the subscription base URL set.
general.twofa_section_hint: Add a second step to local sign-in. Enable any of the methods below (accounts enroll them from My Account); authenticator codes, one-time recovery codes, and an account's enrolled passkey are all available automatically at the login challenge.
general.totp_hint: Lets accounts with a local password set up an authenticator app (TOTP) from My Account. Turning this off only blocks new enrollment — it does not remove the authenticator from already-enrolled accounts. SSO accounts are unaffected.
general.twofa_allow_email_hint: A peer login-challenge method, available once the account has another factor (TOTP / passkey) enrolled. It's weaker — whoever has the password and inbox gets in — so it's off by default.
general.require_2fa_for_staff_hint: When on, an admin/operator without TOTP or a passkey is required to finish setup right after login before using the panel. You can also require it per group (Groups). Local accounts only.
general.local_auth_scope_hint: Two-factor auth and passkeys apply to LOCAL login only. MFA for SSO sign-ins is the identity provider's (IdP's) responsibility — PSP doesn't add its own. An account that has both a local password and an SSO binding is not subject to these when it signs in via SSO.
general.passkey_hint: Lets accounts with a local password register passkeys (WebAuthn) from My Account. With usernameless login on, the login page shows a "Sign in with a passkey" button that needs no username. Requires the subscription base URL to be set (it determines the RP domain) and an HTTPS site (localhost excepted). SSO accounts are unaffected.
general.allow_user_personal_rules: Let users edit their personal rules
general.allow_user_personal_rules_hint: Once disabled, users can still see their personal rules on the self-service page but cannot save changes. Admins are unaffected and can still edit any user's rules manually.
general.timezone_hint: Used for traffic resets, user expire_at math, and the default timezone of the admin traffic chart. Subscription and end-user views always follow the browser's timezone.
general.max_panel_concurrency_hint: Maximum parallel ListInbounds fan-out across 3X-UI panels. 0 uses the built-in default of 8. Raising it does nothing for single-panel deployments; useful only with 5+ panels where 3X-UI has spare CPU. Values above 64 clamp down.
general.auth_event_retention_days_hint: How long login events (including failures) are kept. 0 = never prune. Default 90 (compliance-friendly).
general.traffic_history_days_hint: How far back the chart can render. Older data is auto-pruned. Raw 5-min snapshots are kept for a fixed 7-day window for "today" live refresh; this knob controls the hourly rollup table's retention. 0 = never prune. Default 730 (2y, gives "last 1 year" 1× buffer).
general.expire_before_days: Notify N days before account expires
general.expire_before_days_hint: Email the user this many days before their account's expire_at.
general.traffic_remain_percent_hint: Email the user when their current-period remaining traffic falls below this percentage.
general.emergency_access_quota_gb_hint: 0 = unlimited; once exceeded the panel ends the window early and re-disables the user

geo.hint: Fully offline: resolved from a local .mmdb database — no third-party calls, no caching. Drop the database into
geo.hint2: , or configure auto-update below. Country/region are reliable; city is approximate.
geo.active_db_hint: Which database to use when several are present (blank = first by filename). Only one source is ever active — no merging, no conflicts.
geo.no_db: No .mmdb database found — drop one in, or configure auto-update and click "Update now".
geo.update_interval_hint: Minimum 1 hour; takes effect without a restart.
geo.update_hint: “Update now” saves your settings first, then downloads. MaxMind databases must be refreshed within 30 days (EULA) — enabling auto-update is recommended.
geo.attribution: Attribution required where the data is used: MaxMind (GeoLite2) / DB-IP / IPinfo — depending on the database in use.
geo.update_running: Update still running in the background; refresh later to see the result.

brand.asset_hint: Leave Logo / Icon blank to use built-in defaults.
brand.theme_color_hint: Used as the default for every user. Users can still override via the palette in the top bar. Leave blank to fall back to the built-in default.

sso.reload_error: Saved to the database, but reloading the live service failed: {{error}}
sso.allow_auto_create: Allow accounts to be auto-created on first SSO login
sso.allow_auto_create_hint: When disabled, an SSO sign-in for a UPN that hasn't been pre-created in the panel is redirected to a "contact your administrator" page. IdP admin groups are unaffected.
sso.role_rules_hint: Rules are evaluated in order; the first IdP attribute value match decides the panel role. Empty Attribute = match against the groups attribute. Panel role accepts custom strings (custom roles get user-level access until the panel role enum is extended).
sso.role_rules_empty: No rules configured; SSO won't touch the panel role — all role assignment stays in the panel UI.
sso.role_rules_keep_hint: When on: if THIS rule doesn't match this login AND the user's current role equals this rule's role, leave the role alone. Off means the rule set as a whole decides (demote on miss).
sso.role_rules_note_placeholder: Optional: describe what this rule represents

saml.sp_key: SP private key (PEM, leave blank to keep current)
saml.sp_key_kept: SP private key is configured (kept as-is)
saml.attr_upn_hint: Attribute Name URN that carries the UPN, or "nameid" to read from <Subject><NameID> (default for Okta / Google / Keycloak / ADFS)
saml.fetch_url_required: Please fill in the IdP metadata URL first
saml.fetch_saved: Metadata verified and SAML settings saved
saml.sp_auto_hint: These fields are auto-derived from your panel's public base URL (a self-signed cert is generated on first save). Paste them into your IdP's Service Provider configuration.
saml.sp_save_first: Save once to materialise the SP details

oidc.client_secret: Client secret (leave blank to keep current)

mail.tpl_missing_hint: This template hasn't been initialised yet. Click "Reset to default" to load the default content.
mail.tpl_vars_hint: Uses Go template syntax — {{.FieldName}} for substitution, {{if .FieldName}}...{{end}} for conditional rendering.
mail.reset_confirm_body: The current template will be overwritten by the default. Any customizations will be lost.

portal.no_links: No quick links yet — click below to add one.

announcement.title: Title
announcement.popup: Show as a popup (on first site visit)
announcement.popup_hint: Users can dismiss with "Got it" or check "Don't show again". The dismissed state is stored only in the user's browser; updates to the announcement content trigger the popup again.

subscription.registry_hint: Detection families pick the subscription format by User-Agent; the apps under a family are the portal's one-click import targets. Disabling a family blocks that client from fetching AND hides all its import apps, so a blocked client is never still advertised.
subscription.no_apps: No import apps in this family (detection only)
subscription.reset_rules_confirm_body: Every row in the list will be replaced with the built-in default rules. Custom rules and edits cannot be recovered.
subscription.reset_clients_confirm_body: Every row in the list will be replaced with the built-in default presets. Custom edits cannot be recovered.
subscription.no_rules: No rules yet — click below to add one.
subscription.no_clients: No clients yet — click below to add one.
subscription.sub_import_tutorial_url: Import tutorial URL (powers the 'View tutorial' button on the user portal; hidden when blank)
subscription.sub_profile_name_template_hint: Server-rendered string used for the Content-Disposition / Profile-Title response headers and for the &name= deep-link param. Placeholders: {{ site_title }}, {{ app_title }}, {{ display_name }}, {{ upn }}, {{ user }} (display_name with UPN fallback). Empty falls back to {{ site_title }} - {{ user }}.
subscription.sub_region_flag_prefix: Prefix node names with the region's flag
subscription.sub_region_flag_prefix_hint: When enabled, the subscription renderer prepends the Unicode flag of each node's Region (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code) to the node name. If you already type a flag manually in DisplayName, enabling this will double-flag — strip the manual ones first.
subscription.filter_mode_whitelist: Whitelist (block by default, allow known only)
subscription.filter_mode_help:
Blacklist (default): only clients you explicitly disable are blocked; unknown clients pass through (served mihomo as fallback).

Whitelist: only known + enabled client families are allowed; anything unknown or disabled is blocked and counts as an abuse strike (may trigger auto-disable). Use for strict abuse protection.
subscription.sub_block_auto_disable: Auto-disable account on abusive subscription requests
subscription.sub_block_notify_user: Email the user when a blocked client is used

traffic.title: Traffic
traffic.rank_note: Collection interval comes from system settings (default 5 min).

trend.chart_note: Chart is derived from cumulative snapshot deltas.

templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: A template = client format + bound rule sets. Subscription rendering uses the bindings set here.

placeholder.rule_sets: Pick rule sets matching this template's proxy groups
placeholder.content: Supports proxies / proxy_groups / rules_common / rules_personal placeholders

hint.rule_sets: Subscription rendering only uses the rule sets bound here.
hint.auto_groups: Proxy groups will be auto-generated from the rule targets of the bound rule sets.
hint.missing_targets: Rule targets not found in the template's proxy groups: {{names}}
hint.groups_ok: Detected {{n}} proxy groups; rule targets match.
hint.no_binding: Nothing bound — only nodes, proxy groups, and personal rules will render.
hint.incomplete: Template content is too short to analyze.

confirm.reset_message: Overwrite the on-disk content of {{slug}} with the version embedded in this build. Any customizations will be lost.

templates.cannot_delete_seeded: Seeded templates can't be deleted — use Restore to recover the default content

confirm.purge_message: Purge all non-active (succeeded / canceled) sync task records? Pending / running tasks are unaffected.

logs.title: Logs
logs.retention_hint: 0 = never auto-purge. The current value is used by the 'Purge expired' button.
logs.region_hint: Region is estimated from the offline IP database; city-level is approximate only.

confirm.clear_sub_message: Clear all subscription logs? This cannot be undone.
confirm.clear_audit_message: Clear all audit logs? This cannot be undone.
confirm.clear_email_message: Clear all email logs? This cannot be undone.

rules.title: Rule sets
rules.subtitle: Rule sets are reusable snippets — they only participate in subscription rendering when bound to a template.

hint.proxy_group_order: Optional; one group name per line. Templates binding this set order their proxy groups by this list first; anything not listed keeps the order of first occurrence in the content.

validate.slug_exists: A rule set with slug "{{slug}}" already exists — choose a different slug

confirm.delete_used_suffix: This rule set is referenced by {{names}}. After deletion those templates will skip it.
confirm.reset_message: Overwrite the on-disk content of {{slug}} with the version embedded in this build. Any customizations will be lost.

rules.cannot_delete_seeded: Seeded rule sets can't be deleted — use Restore to recover the default content
rules.duplicate: Duplicate as a new rule set

cert_source.from_panel_filled: Filled in the panel's cert paths
cert_source.from_panel_unsupported: Panel too old (needs 3X-UI 3.2.7+); fill the paths manually
cert_source.bound: Bound the managed certificate and deployed it
cert_source.managed_help: PSP deploys this certificate to the node and auto-renews it.
cert_source.no_active_certs: No active certificates — issue one on the Certificates page first.

nodes.title: Nodes
nodes.subtitle: Manage 3X-UI inbounds; subscription rendering follows the enabled state set here.
nodes.managed_search_placeholder: Search display name / server / address / region / tags / ID…
nodes.managed_search_disables_drag: Drag-to-reorder is disabled while filtering; clear the search to re-enable
nodes.unmanaged_pick_server: Select a server to view its unmanaged inbounds
nodes.unmanaged_load_failed: Failed to load this server's inbounds

create_separator_dialog.title: New separator
create_separator_dialog.hint: Separators render as a DIRECT proxy in client node lists for visual grouping only. They don't participate in 3X-UI, traffic accounting, or health checks. Which groups display them is controlled by the "Show in all groups" toggle + group picker below — not by node tag_filter.

claim_dialog.title: Claim existing client
claim_dialog.subtitle: Link an existing 3X-UI client (identified by email) on inbound #{{id}} to a panel user. Common for migrating legacy inbounds.
claim_dialog.client_uuid: Client UUID (optional; leave blank to keep the existing 3X-UI value)
claim_dialog.validate_required: Please pick a user and enter the client email

edit_inbound_dialog.title: Edit inbound config
edit_inbound_dialog.unsupported: Visual editor only supports VLESS / SS-2022 inbounds.

create_dialog.title: New inbound
create_dialog.note: Simplified: VLESS + Reality + TCP only. Other protocols coming next iteration.
create_dialog.note_multi: Supports VLESS (TCP / WS / gRPC × None / TLS / Reality) and Shadowsocks 2022. Fields appear based on the selected protocol.
create_dialog.no_servers: Please add a 3X-UI server on the Servers page first.
create_dialog.advanced_hint: Edit 3X-UI's settings / streamSettings / sniffing JSON directly. Paste any field 3X-UI supports (mKCP, h2, splithttp, httpupgrade, etc.); it's sent as-is on save. Turning advanced mode off does NOT backfill the form above — switch with care.
create_dialog.validate_required: Display name / address / region are required
create_dialog.validate_reality_keys: Generate or fill in Reality privateKey / publicKey / shortId
create_dialog.validate_reality_target: Reality dest and SNI are required
create_dialog.validate_ss2022: SS-2022 cipher and PSK are required

field.separator_mode_node_bound: Bound to nodes (show when any linked node appears)
field.separator_mode_hint_node_bound: Only visible when the rendered group contains at least one of the linked nodes. Position is still set by drag-to-reorder.
field.separator_node_ids_hint: On subscription render, any group whose user can see at least one of these nodes shows the separator. Leave empty to hide it everywhere.

confirm.delete_message: Delete node {{name}}? This removes the corresponding inbound from 3X-UI.
confirm.detach_message: Detach node {{name}}? The panel will drop the node record and forget its clients locally — 3X-UI will NOT be contacted. Any clients Passwall Panel previously created on this inbound stay on 3X-UI for you to clean up there. Use this when the upstream server is offline or decommissioned; otherwise use Delete.

field.account_self_locked: You cannot disable your own account

role.help_admin: Full access: servers, SSO, mail SMTP, rule sets / templates, audit clear, can create any role.
role.help_operator: Day-to-day: user CRUD, traffic, emergency access, toggle nodes, audit read, sync tasks. Cannot touch servers / system settings / SSO / mail SMTP, cannot change roles, cannot modify admin or operator accounts.
role.help_user: Regular user: only the /user/me self-service portal (subscription, usage, emergency access, change password, personal rules).

result.intro: User {{upn}} created. Record the initial password — this is shown only once.

validate.demote_self: You are demoting the currently signed-in admin account to a regular user. After saving you will lose admin access. Continue?

toast.renew_permanent_skip: Permanent accounts don't need quick renewal; edit the user to set an expiry first.

confirm.delete_message: Delete user {{upn}}? This removes their client from every 3X-UI inbound.
confirm.reset_credentials_message: Resetting {{upn}}'s credentials immediately invalidates the old subscription URL and forces existing connections to drop. Continue?
confirm.unlink_sso_message: {{upn}} is currently bound to {{provider}}. After unlinking, the next SSO sign-in from this person re-binds the account to the IdP identity in use at that moment. Continue?
confirm.batch_unlink_sso_message: Unlink the SSO binding for {{names}}{{suffix}} — {{count}} users in total? Their next SSO sign-in will re-bind to whatever identity the IdP returns at that moment.
confirm.batch_delete_message: Delete {{names}}{{suffix}}? This removes their clients from every 3X-UI inbound.
confirm.reset_2fa_message: This clears {{upn}}'s authenticator app (TOTP) and its recovery codes; their passkeys, if any, are unaffected. They'll be able to sign in with just their password (unless another factor remains) and will need to re-enroll. Use only when the user lost their authenticator and has no recovery codes.

security.subtitle: Admins can reset / revoke / regenerate credentials, but can't enroll 2FA or passkeys for a user (that needs their own device).
security.twofa_none_hint: This user hasn't enabled two-factor auth.
security.regen_recovery_message: This invalidates all of {{upn}}'s current recovery codes and issues a new set. Relay the new codes to the user over a secure channel — they're shown only once.
security.regen_recovery_done: New recovery codes (shown once — relay to the user):

reset_password.title: Reset login password
reset_password.hint: Leave blank to generate a random password, or use the dice button to auto-fill then edit.

batch.no_renewable: None of the selected users have a renewable expiry.
batch.unlink_sso_none_eligible: None of the selected users are bound to an SSO identity

rules.title: Personal rules — {{upn}}
rules.placeholder: One rule per line; mihomo / sing-box syntax

passkeys.title: Passkeys
passkeys.intro: View and revoke this user's passkeys (WebAuthn). You can't enroll one on their behalf — that needs the user's own authenticator.
passkeys.empty: This user has no registered passkeys.
passkeys.revoke_message: This revokes {{upn}}'s passkey "{{name}}". That device can no longer be used to sign in.
passkeys.revoke_all_message: This revokes all {{count}} of {{upn}}'s passkeys. Use only when the account lost every device or is suspected compromised.

field.name: Name
field.require_2fa: Require this group's members to set up two-factor auth

scope.title: Per-group overrides (else inherit global)
scope.sub_region_flag: Region flag prefix on node names
scope.save_error: Some overrides failed to save — reopen to verify
scope.create_hint: Save the group first, then set per-group overrides here.

members.hint: Filter by this group under Users to view and manage members.

placeholder.tags: Pick region:XX / tag:YY from the dropdown, or type custom

hint.mode: AND = every condition must match; OR = at least one matches
hint.tags: Pick existing regions/tags from the dropdown, or type a custom condition (e.g. server:foo).
hint.custom_conditions: Comma-separated. For conditions the three boxes above don't cover, e.g. vendor:gcp.

warn.has_members: This group still has {{count}} member(s); move them out first.

certs.page_subtitle: Issue and auto-renew wildcard TLS certificates via ACME DNS-01.
certs.acme_email_hint: Let's Encrypt contact email (used for expiry notices; may be left blank)
certs.acme_directory_hint: Defaults to Let's Encrypt production; use staging for testing to avoid rate limits
certs.acct_subtitle: Manage multiple CA accounts (different CAs / contact emails / EAB credentials); each certificate picks the one to issue under.
certs.acct_empty: No ACME accounts yet. Add one before issuing a certificate.
certs.acct_eab_hint: External Account Binding (EAB) — required by ZeroSSL, Google Public CA, etc.; leave blank for Let's Encrypt.
certs.acct_delete_msg: Delete "{{name}}"? Certificates still using it must switch to another account first.
certs.secret_keep: Set — leave blank to keep unchanged
certs.renew_subtitle: Auto-renew threshold and check frequency (applies to all certificates).
certs.cred_edit_keep_hint: Leave a field blank to keep its stored value.
certs.title: Certificates
certs.need_cred_first: Add a DNS credential below before you can issue a certificate.
certs.need_acct_first: Add an ACME account on the "ACME accounts" tab before you can issue a certificate.
certs.need_acct_and_cred: Before issuing a certificate, add a CA account on the "ACME accounts" tab and a DNS credential on the "DNS credentials" tab.
certs.validation_required: Please fill in the required fields
certs.validation_secret_required: Every credential value must be filled in
certs.create_queued: Issuance queued; processing in the background
certs.domains_help: One domain per line / comma-separated; wildcards like *.example.com are supported
certs.progress_queued: Queued — waiting for the issuance worker.
certs.provider_help: Pick a DNS provider (searchable), e.g. cloudflare / alidns / dnspod; use exec / httpreq for others
certs.cred_kv_help: Enter the env vars from the provider's lego docs, e.g. Cloudflare: CF_DNS_API_TOKEN

servers.title: Servers (3X-UI)
servers.subtitle: Inbounds (nodes) are attached to a server. New nodes pick from this list.

placeholder.name: default / us-west / ...
placeholder.password: API Token preferred; password as fallback

hint.name: Display name; the internal reference uses the database ID.

compat.banner_title_too_old: {{count}} server(s) running a 3X-UI version below Passwall Panel's minimum requirement
compat.banner_body_too_old: Affected servers: {{panels}}. Please upgrade these servers as soon as possible to restore traffic poll, reconcile, and other core functions.
compat.banner_title_untested: {{count}} server(s) running a 3X-UI version outside Passwall Panel's tested range
compat.banner_body_untested: Affected servers: {{panels}}. These servers may work normally, or may fail unexpectedly on schema incompatibility. Check whether Passwall Panel has a newer release, or open an issue in the repository to request validation of this 3X-UI version.

validate.credentials_required: Please configure an API token or username/password first.

toast.upgrade_panel_started: 3X-UI upgrade to {{target}} initiated; Passwall Panel will run a smoke probe in ~60s and log success/failure to the audit trail.
toast.upgrade_panel_already_latest: Already on the latest version ({{version}}); nothing to upgrade.
toast.upgrade_panel_blocked: Upgrade refused: target {{latest}} is outside Passwall Panel's tested range (highest {{max}}). Upgrade Passwall Panel first.
toast.batch_upgrade_xray_ok: Upgraded Xray on {{count}} server(s) to latest
toast.batch_upgrade_panel_ok: Initiated 3X-UI upgrade on {{count}} server(s); each runs a smoke probe in ~60s (results in the audit log)
toast.batch_upgrade_panel_ok_latest: Initiated 3X-UI upgrade on {{count}} server(s); {{latest}} already on the latest version (skipped)
toast.batch_upgrade_panel_partial: Initiated {{ok}}; {{latest}} already latest; {{blocked}} blocked (target outside tested range — force per-panel); {{fail}} failed

confirm.delete_message: Delete server {{name}}? The server must have no nodes referencing it.
confirm.batch_delete_message: Delete {{names}}{{suffix}}? These servers must have no nodes referencing them.
confirm.batch_upgrade_xray_message: Install the latest xray-core on {{count}} server(s) in parallel. Each 3X-UI resolves its own latest tag, so versions may differ by a patch. The panel itself stays up; only the xray child process restarts. Continue?
confirm.batch_upgrade_panel_message: Fire a 3X-UI self-upgrade to latest on {{count}} panel(s). Each panel restarts — every user on it drops for ~30–60s — and each runs a smoke probe ~60s later (results in the audit log). Panels whose target version is outside Passwall Panel's tested range are blocked (not forced) — to force, use the per-panel ⋮ menu. Continue?
confirm.upgrade_panel_message: Passwall Panel will check the target version first and only fire the upgrade on {{name}} if it's within the tested range. The panel will restart; Passwall Panel runs a smoke probe ~60s later. Continue?
confirm.upgrade_force_title: Target version not verified by Passwall Panel
confirm.upgrade_force_message: About to upgrade to {{latest}}. Passwall Panel's highest verified 3X-UI version is {{max}}. Forcing the upgrade may cause traffic poll, reconcile, and other critical flows to fail due to protocol or field changes. Recommend upgrading Passwall Panel to a release that supports this 3X-UI version first. Force upgrade anyway?
confirm.upgrade_xray_message: Install xray-core on {{name}}. Pick a target version below (defaults to latest). The panel itself stays up; only the xray child process restarts.
